commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Did I then begin to inquire of God for him. be it far from me: let not the king impute any thing unto his servant, nor to all the house of my father: for your servant knew nothing of all this, less or more. We might read without an interrogation, "I have to-day begun. "(Chaldean) never suspecting that it would be disagreeable to the king. (Calmet) His character and his declarations, led me to conclude quite the contrary, so that I cannot lawfully be accused of any conspiracy.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
